evals.prompt: How do I create a REST API endpoint in Next.js App Router?
evals.expected_output: Create app/api/users/route.ts and export named functions GET, POST, etc. Use NextRequest and NextResponse. Parse the request body with await req.json(), validate with Zod safeParse, and return NextResponse.json(data, { status: 201 }). For dynamic segments, add [id]/route.ts and receive params as a Promise<{ id: string }> in the second argument.

evals.prompt: How do I redirect unauthenticated users at the edge in Next.js?
evals.expected_output: Use middleware.ts at the project root. Export a middleware function that reads cookies or headers, then returns NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', request.url)) for protected paths. Export a config object with a matcher array to limit which paths trigger the middleware. Keep middleware lightweight — no database calls, it runs at the Edge.

evals.prompt: How do I access environment variables safely in Next.js?
evals.expected_output: Only NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefixed variables are exposed to the browser — everything else is server-only. Use @t3-oss/env-nextjs with Zod to validate env vars at startup: createEnv({ server: { DATABASE_URL: z.string().url() }, client: { NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: z.string() }, runtimeEnv: process.env }). This catches missing env vars at build time with type safety.
